Details:
Reverse osmosis (RO) was able to remove 85 to 93 percent of carbofuran in water based on the results of one bench-scale study using multiple membranes (cellulose acetate and polyamide) at pressures from 160 to 200 psi membranes in a single-pass arrangement.  In pilot-scale studies using polyamide membranes, 76 to 90 percent removal of carbofuran was achieved.
In EPA Environmental Technology Verification assessments, RO membranes controlled greater than 98 percent of carbofuran.
